commit.message:
Add containerized worker runtime and switch TODO format to plain bullets (#9)

* Add two-layer structure: installable scaffold + autonomous worker runtime

- Add worker/ — Dockerfile (node:20-slim + Claude Code CLI + gh CLI),
  entrypoint.sh (cron job model: clone/pull → run Claude → exit),
  worker-instructions.md (built-in instructions, per-repo override supported),
  README.md (Docker Desktop + Kubernetes deployment reference)
- Add .github/workflows/build-worker.yml — builds and publishes worker
  image to GHCR when worker/ or scripts/ change on main
- Add scaffold/specs/ — template source files for the installable payload
  (separate from this repo's live spec content)
- Add scripts/generate-dist.sh — copies scaffold sources + commands into
  dist/ with auto-generated do-not-edit headers
- Add dist/ — generated scaffold payload committed for downstream consumption
- Add .gitignore — exclude .claude/settings.local.json
- Fill in specs/spec.md with current two-layer system state
- Add specs/scaffold.todo.md and specs/worker.todo.md (filed from issues #7/#8)
- Update README.md with "Running on autopilot" section
- Update CHANGELOG.md WIP entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Switch TODO format from checkboxes to plain bullets; add proximity principle

- Replace `- [ ]` checkbox format with plain `- ` bullets across all todo files
  and the knock-out-todos command: remove Step 0 orphan scan, update grep
  pattern, atomic remove-and-promote flow replaces check-then-move
- Add PHILOSOPHY.md "Proximity shapes behavior" section using the checkbox
  incident as a concrete example
- Add /respec Update step 5: detect TODO format changes and offer migration
  with user approval; add corresponding Ideas item to spec.todo.md
- Remove orphaned [x] items from worker.todo.md (already in spec.md)
- Add CONTRIBUTING.md documenting scaffold source layout and dist/ workflow
- Regenerate dist/ to include all command and template updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Copilot review: dedup, non-destructive copy, format fixes, version pin

- Fix bootstrap PR spam: use deterministic branch 'scaffold/bootstrap';
  check for open PR before creating, exit early if one exists
- Fix install mode: rsync --ignore-existing so existing repo files
  (e.g. .github/workflows/) are never overwritten; add rsync to Dockerfile
- Fix /intake Path 1: emit plain '- ' bullets, not '- [ ]' checkboxes
- Fix dep TODO template (scaffold/specs/deps/README.md): plain bullets
- Fix knock-out-todos wording: "unchecked TODOs" -> "open TODO items"
- Remove undocumented EXECUTION_MODE from Dockerfile comment and spec.md
- Pin @anthropic-ai/claude-code to 2.1.71 to reduce supply chain risk
- Regenerate dist/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make branch-before-work explicit in worker instructions

The vague principle "open PRs rather than pushing to main" wasn't enough
— the agent needs to be told to create a branch before touching any files.
Add a "Before you start" section with an explicit git checkout command,
and update "On completion" to push the branch and open a PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix stale branch handling, CI trigger gap, and stale docs

- entrypoint.sh: after confirming no open PR, clean up any stale
  local/remote scaffold/bootstrap branch before recreating it, so
  repeated runs are idempotent even after a closed previous attempt
- build-worker.yml: add dist/** to trigger paths so image rebuilds
  when scaffold sources change and dist/ is regenerated
- worker/README.md, specs/spec.md: update stale references from
  scaffold/bootstrap-* (timestamped) to scaffold/bootstrap (deterministic)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use * bullets in Reminders so grep for ^- naturally skips them

Removes the need for the "skip ## Reminders" caveat in the grep
instruction — the formatting itself enforces the distinction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add scaffold.md and worker.md; trim spec.md to system overview

Each feature TODO file now has a corresponding current-state spec:
- specs/scaffold.md — Layer 1 (installed files, dist/ generation)
- specs/worker.md  — Layer 2 (container, modes, CI/CD)

specs/spec.md is now a brief system overview that links to both.
Reminders in scaffold.todo.md and worker.todo.md updated to point
to their respective spec files instead of the generic spec.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Updated changelog

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

commit.message:
fix(feature-svc): fix CORS loading all origins from config

The old code manually read CORS__ALLOWEDORIGINS__0 env var and replaced
the entire origins array with just that single value. This broke CORS
for any other domains (e.g. www.aivalid.id, localhost:3000).

Now uses standard ASP.NET Core configuration binding which correctly
merges env var overrides (CORS__ALLOWEDORIGINS__0, __1, __2) with
appsettings.json array entries.

Logs configured origins at startup for easier debugging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resume Fabric capacity before workspace deletion in teardown

Workspace deletion fails with 400 when capacity is paused. The script
now auto-discovers the capacity from the RG, resumes it, waits 10s,
then deletes the workspace. Falls back to manual instructions if still
failing.

fix: use base64 data URIs instead of webview resource URIs for PNG assets

Webview resource URIs (vscode-resource:) have been unreliable for
new Image() loads in VS Code webviews — failures are silent and vary
by VS Code version/platform. After 5+ rounds of fixes (CORS retry,
GC prevention, crossOrigin removal), the fundamental issue persisted.

This change reads PNG files in the extension host, encodes them as
base64 data URIs (data:image/png;base64,...), and sends those through
postMessage instead of webview resource URIs. Data URIs:
- Are explicitly allowed by the CSP (img-src data:)
- Bypass the webview resource server entirely
- Work reliably with new Image().src in all browsers
- Match how pixel-agents (reference impl) handles assets

commit.message:
fix(feature-svc): fix CORS and rate limiting middleware order

Critical bugs fixed:
- CORS middleware was running AFTER rate limiting, so 429 responses
  had no Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers. Browsers blocked these
  as network errors, showing 'Unable to connect to Feature Service'
- Rate limiter ran before auth, so user_id JWT claim was unavailable
  and Financial policy fell back to per-IP (all users shared 10 req/60s)
- Production .env had CORS__ALLOWEDORIGINS__0=https://www.aivalid.id

Middleware order: CORS → Auth → Rate Limiting (was: Rate → CORS → Auth)
Rate limit: GET/HEAD on financial endpoints now use Global policy (100/60s)

commit.message:
fix: ICC mismatch variants now produce output files

encodeImageWithICCProfile() returned nil when CGColorSpaceCreateWithICCData
rejected malformed/synthetic ICC data, causing 0 mismatch files to be generated.

Changes:
- Add fallback encoding in encodeImageWithICCProfile() — encodes image as-is
  when color space creation fails, ensuring output files are always created
- Rewrite encodeImageWithMismatchedProfile() strategies 0-1 to use named color
  spaces (Display P3, AdobeRGB 1998) for real gamut/gamma mismatch on sRGB content
- Strategies 2-3 (Lab abstract, truncated) use synthetic ICC headers that trigger
  the new fallback path
- Result: 32 mismatch files per run (was 0), total output 152 → 184 files

Tested: native ASAN+UBSAN build, 0 sanitizer errors, all 19 seeds produce
complete variant sets (base + no-ICC + mismatch across PNG/TIFF formats)

commit.message:
fix(set_pps_rate): correct CFG-VALSET key IDs, units, and locked params

The previous commit used wrong key IDs (group 0x51 = I2C config instead
of 0x05 = timepulse), wrong LEN_TP1 ID, and nanoseconds instead of µs.

Correct key IDs from u-blox M10 ICD / SparkFun GNSS library:
  0x40050002  CFG-TP-PERIOD_TP1        (U4, µs, unlocked)
  0x40050003  CFG-TP-PERIOD_LOCK_TP1  (U4, µs, locked to GNSS)
  0x40050004  CFG-TP-LEN_TP1          (U4, µs, unlocked)
  0x40050005  CFG-TP-LEN_LOCK_TP1     (U4, µs, locked to GNSS)
  0x10050007  CFG-TP-TP1_ENA          (U1, bool)
  0x1005000b  CFG-TP-POL_TP1          (U1, bool: 1 = rising)
  0x20050030  CFG-TP-PULSE_LENGTH_DEF (U1: 1 = absolute µs length)

Split into three ≤32-byte VALSET frames (period, length, enable/mode).
Revert public API from nanoseconds back to microseconds (µs matches ICD).
Update interrupt.rs call site accordingly.
